name: dateConvert
description: Converts local time to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), or UTC to local time. The function uses the daylight savings settings in the executing computer to compute daylight savings time, if required.

params.name: conversionType
params.description:
`local2Utc` : Converts local time to UTC time.
`utc2Local` : Converts UTC time to local time.

params.name: date
params.description: Date and time string or a variable that contains one. To create, use `createDateTime()` or `now()`. When passing a date/time object as a string, enclose it in quotes or it will be interpreted as a numeric representation of a date/time object.

coldfusion.notes: Member function is available in CF11+.

lucee.notes: Warning: This function only exists for compatibility reasons and should not be used. It is no longer supported in Lucee 5+. The timezone is not part of the datetime object. The function simply add/subtracts the time between the local time and the UTC from the date Object.

examples.title: Converting UTC to Local
examples.description: This example makes sense only if your server time is UTC. now() uses your server settings when creating a datetime object.

examples.title: Using the Member Function
examples.description: This demonstrates how to use the member function on a created date object.
